feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Optical outburst evolution of the transient black hole X-ray binary Swift J1727. 8-1613: Disc response to jet ejections and late-outburst emergence of powerful disc ...
entry.content:
Authors: N Castro Segura, K Solomons, JM Corral-Santana... -arXive-prints, 2026

Abstract: Swift J1727. 8$-$1613 is a newly discovered transient low-mass X-ray binary harbouring a stellar-mass ($\sim 10M_\odot $) black hole. We present state-resolved VLT/X-Shooter optical spectroscopy of its 2023 outburst, sampling the luminous hard-to-soft ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Contractions of the relativistic quantum LCT group and the emergence of spacetime symmetries
entry.content:
Authors: A Feno Hasina Rasamimanana... -arXive-prints, 2026

Abstract: Advances in the study of relativistic quantum phase space have established the set of Linear Canonical Transformations (LCTs) as a candidate for the fundamental symmetry group associated with relativistic quantum physics. In this framework, for a ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Emergence of volume-law scaling for entanglement negativity from the Hawking radiation of analogue black holes
entry.content:
Authors: S Mahesh Chandran, UR Fischer -arXive-prints, 2026

Abstract: The quantum information content of Hawking radiation holds the key to understanding black-hole evaporation and the fate of unitarity. Motivated by recent advances in cold-atom experiments, we develop a lattice-regularization approach ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: The Structure of Scientific Socialism: Quantum Emergence , Frustration, and the Non-Dual Dialectic
entry.content:
Authors: S Barman Roy -arXive-prints, 2026

Abstract: Classical Marxism and the algebra of revolution were formulated within the ontological constraints of 19th-century Newtonian materialism-a world of discrete, predictable, billiard-ball interactions. However, the 20th-century transitions in physics ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Emergence of the Partial Trace from Classical Probability Theory
entry.content:
Authors: A Macho Ortiz, FJ Fraile Peláez, J Capmany -arXive-prints, 2026

Abstract: The partial trace is commonly introduced in quantum mechanics as an algebraic operation used to define reduced states of composite systems. However, the probabilistic origin of this operation goes systematically unnoticed in the literature ...

entry.title: Searching for Life as We Don't Know It: Detecting Signatures of Planetary-scale Complexity in Exoplanet Atmospheric Spectra
entry.content:
Authors: S Walker, E Janin, E Shkolnik, L Slocombe - ... Society of the Pacific Conference Series, 2026

Abstract: ... life on exoplanets is a central challenge of 21st-century astronomy. We present a new, observation-driven framework for life detection that applies Assembly Theory to exoplanet ... worlds and simulated archetypal exoplanets, including Hot Jupiters ...

entry.title: The JWST Search for Earth-Luna Analogs: Upper Limits on Exomoons and Refined Ephemerides for TOI 700 d and e
entry.content:
Authors: EK Pass, D Charbonneau, A Vanderburg,JL Bean- arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.05235, 2026

Abstract: While no conclusive detections of exomoons have been reported to date, planet formation theories predict that satellites should be a common outcome of the collisional dynamics in early extrasolar systems. Such satellites have the potential to ...

entry.title: Correction to:'The prebiotic emergence of biological evolution'Kocher and Dill

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: C Kocher, K Dill - Royal Society Open Science, 2026

Abstract: There is a typo in our article,'The prebiotic emergence of biological evolution.'R. Soc. Open Sci. 11: 240431 (https://doi. org/10.1098/rsos. 240431). In Equation (B 1), the rate equation for monomer (⁠ du 1/dt=...⁠) had the wrong sign on the term ...
